runs.notes: WebFetch returned 403 (consistent with prior runs); all enrichment + late-breaking research done via WebSearch (8 queries) plus 1 failed WebFetch. All 5 of yesterday-pm's items kept and enriched — no drops; the agentic-coding-tools cluster is still the live narrative. Enrichments: (1) Anthropic $950B raise — added Dario Amodei's May developer-conference figures ($44B annualized revenue, up from $30B announced April 7; inference gross margins 38%->70%+) and the end-of-May expected close; Bloomberg reported the round at $900B on May 12 before NYT walked it to $950B; updated why_it_matters and brief. (2) GitHub Copilot App — added preview-rollout progress (Pro/Pro+ -> Business/Enterprise) and an early dogfooding signal (a team merged 368 PRs through the app over a 30-day window, predating the public preview). (3) Codex Windows sandbox — clarified the native Windows sandbox first shipped March 2026 for PowerShell environments and the May 14 post is the architecture writeup; noted recent edge-case hardening (pseudoconsole startup, elevated runner processes, named-pipe validation). (4) Claude for Small Business — added the one-month Claude Max attendee perk, Tenex.co as tour operator, the free 'AI Fluency for Small Business' course / 4D Framework, and PayPal's parallel 'Close the AI Gap' partnership; corrected the tour-partner attribution from 'co-developed with PayPal' to Tenex.co. (5) Cursor Bugbot — added Cursor's published per-run economics ($1.00-$1.50/run; ~0.7 bugs on Default with 79%+ resolved at merge, ~0.95 on High), annual-sub grandfathering through May 2027, and dashboard-rollout friction reported on the Cursor forum; updated why_it_matters and brief. Late-breaking pass added one item: xAI Grok Build (Tier 2, US) — early-beta coding agent and CLI launched May 15, runs up to 8 parallel agents (plan/search/build), 'Arena Mode' has agents compete and ranks outputs (confirmed but not yet live in beta), local-first, npm install, runs on grok-code-fast-1 (70.8% SWE-Bench Verified, 256k context), SuperGrok Heavy only at $299/mo ($99/mo intro for 6 months); fourth dedicated agentic-coding surface this month and the multi-agent-orchestration bet. Considered but did not add as a second late-breaking item: Google Cloud Next TPU 8t/8i (April 22, three weeks stale); Gemini Omni pre-I/O coverage (still speculation, Google I/O is May 19, declined by prior runs); Thinking Machines interaction models (already in 2026-05-15-am.json as TML-Interaction-Small); Airia Form Review Step (Tier 3, enterprise document extraction, not relevant for Gabe). Snapshot at 6 items: T1=1, T2=5, T3=0. Hero images uniformly null (no WebFetch).

WebFetch returned 403 on anthropic.com/news (consistent with prior runs); all sweeps done via WebSearch (15 queries). Today's 2026-05-15-am.json existed with 11 enriched/refreshed items — applied promotion rule (default exclude). Five fresh PM items shipped: T1=1, T2=4, T3=0. Items: (1) Anthropic in talks for $30-50B raise at $950B valuation — would top OpenAI for first time, bundled with Ramp May AI Index showing Anthropic at 34.4% of US business AI spend vs OpenAI at 32.3% (first crossover); NYT mid-May reporting walked the upper bound from $900B (TC April 29) to $950B; VentureBeat names three threats that could erase the lead; Inc./AI Insider tie the three Anthropic threads (adoption crossover, Small Business launch, $950B raise) into one consolidated story. (2) GitHub Copilot App technical preview May 14 — standalone desktop app for Windows/macOS/Linux, three primitives: Parallel Sessions (per-agent git work trees), Agent Merge (auto-fixes PR-lifecycle friction), Repository Maintenance (NL prompt spawns sub-agents in parallel work trees); Pro/Pro+ early access immediate, Business/Enterprise rolling through the week; GitHub Changelog + DevOps Journal + Neowin + Windows Report coverage. (3) OpenAI Codex Windows sandbox May 14 — two dedicated local accounts (CodexSandboxOffline / CodexSandboxOnline), OS-level firewall boundary at the kernel layer (not application), closes the gap since Codex team joined MSFT in Sept 2025; OpenAI engineering post + Running Codex Safely companion + WinBuzzer + StartupHub coverage; three Microsoft-aligned agentic-coding products (Copilot App, Codex Windows, VS 2026 skills) landing in the same week is the through-line. (4) Claude for Small Business May 13 — 15 prebuilt agentic workflows, 15 reusable skills, 8 connectors (QuickBooks/PayPal/HubSpot/Canva/DocuSign/GWorkspace/M365/Slack), 10-city free workshop tour starting May 14 Chicago; the 'skill' abstraction now spans Claude Code, VS 2026, and a non-engineering product line; Anthropic/TechCrunch/SiliconANGLE/Inc/Axios/9to5Mac/Fast Company. (5) Cursor Bugbot usage-based billing + effort levels May 14 — drops $40/seat/month, switches to pure usage-based at June 8 renewal, three effort levels (Default/High/Custom); 'high effort' finds 35% more bugs at same 80% merge-time resolve rate per Cursor's internal benchmark; third agentic-coding tool this month to restructure billing (after Anthropic Agent SDK split June 15 and Claude Code 50% weekly boost). No promotion from am applied — all 11 am items are deepening but no afternoon news materially changed any of them. Hero images uniformly null (no WebFetch). Considered but did not include: xAI Grok legacy model retirement executing 12pm PT today (refresh already declined this — execution of prior announcement, not new news); OpenAI EU access for GPT-5.5-Cyber + Anthropic Mythos still restricted (May 11, four days stale); Sierra $950M raise at $15.8B (May 4, stale); CAISI/Microsoft/Google/xAI testing agreements (May 5, ten days stale); Nathan Lambert 'How Open Model Ecosystems Compound' (May 12, three days stale and thought-piece rather than news); Gemini Omni pre-I/O leaks (already declined in prior runs, I/O is May 19 and these are speculation); Mistral Medium 3.5 / Vibe remote agents (May 1, two weeks stale).
